title: Durability of the Spear of Longinus
display_title: Durability of the Spear of Longinus
wikitext:
[]: Durability of the Spear of Longinus             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Sat Aug 04, 2012 6:55 am I posed this question in the last topic I created, "Spear of Longinus behavior in EoE." Since no one commented on it and the thread has gone dormant, I thought I'd bring it up in a new thread. As a weapon meant to subdue beings of immense power, I think it's safe to assume that the Spear of Longinus can take a serious beating. We know that Adam's Spear managed to survive Second Impact, an explosion of terrifying intensity, without so much as a scratch on it. But we are also told that Lilith's Spear was "lost" or possibly even "destroyed" during Giant Impact. I don't know how much energy is released when an Egg of Life, a heavenly body the size of a small moon, crashes into a planet, but I'd say that Second Impact is at least comparable to it. Also, I'm going to assume that Lilith's Spear was inside the Egg with Lilith herself at the time of Giant Impact, and so would have been shielded at least somewhat by the Egg's outer shell. tl;dr How is it that one Spear managed to survive Second Impact while another was potentially destroyed by an event of similar magnitude? Any thoughts?

[]:             Quote Post by Jakobramsey » Sat Aug 04, 2012 11:25 am Well, apparently the Spears are supposed to have a sort of "will," or consciousness. Adam was the first to land on earth, but then shortly after, Lilith landed as well, resulting in the first impact as you know. The wiki states (if I read it correctly) that in the First Impact, Lilith's spear was destroyed and Adam's "took the initiative to subdue its own counterpart Seed," aka Adam. I wonder why it would do that. Perhaps it felt it should be subservient to the guest. Maybe there's just something I'm missing, but it would make far more sense to me if Lilith's spear, instead of being destroyed, attacked Adam's spear, destroying it, and then subdued Adam, so that Lilith could be the presiding life form on the planet. This would mean that what we know as Adam's spear is actually Lilith's. (feel free to refute this). Another possibility is neither spear was destroyed, but Seele took it like it did the sensitive material within the Dead Sea Scrolls, and nobody, not even Gendo, knew about it. However, the only real evidence I could think of to support this is the fact that I can't think of anything contradictory off the top of my head.

[]:             Quote Post by Eliaskar » Sat Aug 04, 2012 2:04 pm Jakobramsey wrote: Well, apparently the Spears are supposed to have a sort of "will," or consciousness. Adam was the first to land on earth, but then shortly after, Lilith landed as well, resulting in the first impact as you know. The wiki states (if I read it correctly) that in the First Impact, Lilith's spear was destroyed and Adam's "took the initiative to subdue its own counterpart Seed," aka Adam. I wonder why it would do that. Perhaps it felt it should be subservient to the guest. Maybe there's just something I'm missing, but it would make far more sense to me if Lilith's spear, instead of being destroyed, attacked Adam's spear, destroying it, and then subdued Adam, so that Lilith could be the presiding life form on the planet. This would mean that what we know as Adam's spear is actually Lilith's. (feel free to refute this). Another possibility is neither spear was destroyed, but Seele took it like it did the sensitive material within the Dead Sea Scrolls, and nobody, not even Gendo, knew about it. However, the only real evidence I could think of to support this is the fact that I can't think of anything contradictory off the top of my head.

[]:             Quote Post by Jakobramsey » Sat Aug 04, 2012 3:00 pm Eliaskar wrote: I always thought that since Lilith's was destroyed on impact then since Adam's spear was already with him it basically thought "Okay, two Seeds aren't allowed on one planet, i'm with one right now so i'l subdue this one."

[]:             Quote Post by Der Kommissar » Sat Aug 04, 2012 3:11 pm The theorized impact that created our moon in real life was a collision between a Mars-sized body and early Earth, as a I recall. Some googling and wokring backwards would probably tell you readily an absolute minimum of energy that it takes to bring a spear offline. "A metric fuckload" is a fair estimate.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Aug 04, 2012 5:08 pm The CI says that Lilith's Spear 'may have escaped from [her] and dispersed during First Impact', then goes on to say that it was probably destroyed because it hasn't been found. Well, that's kind of vague. How did it 'escape, disperse', and somehow get itself destroyed, exactly...?

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Sun Aug 05, 2012 2:53 am Der Kommissar wrote: "A metric fuckload" is a fair estimate.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Sun Aug 05, 2012 3:02 am New idea: Lilith is a "rogue" Seed of Life that has no Spear. SEELE and NERV just assume that every Seed has a corresponding Spear, so they would obviously conclude that Lilith's Spear was knocked away or destroyed during Giant Impact. They have no way of knowing what actually happened.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Aug 05, 2012 5:01 am Except the CI tells us this, and SEELE is basing its information on what's in the SDSS. They say each Seed has a LoL, so it's safe to assume that's correct unless we're told differently elsewhere. As to the impacts: one cut Earth in half and created the moon, while the other "just" blew up Antarctica. The one is much more powerful than the other. If 2I had had the same magnitude as 1I the Earth would have broken apart.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Sun Aug 05, 2012 5:32 am Bagheera wrote: As to the impacts: one cut Earth in half and created the moon, while the other "just" blew up Antarctica. The one is much more powerful than the other. If 2I had had the same magnitude as 1I the Earth would have broken apart.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Aug 05, 2012 5:59 am riffraff11235 wrote: I wouldn't say Giant Impact "cut the Earth in half." Current models say that the impacting body embedded itself in the Earth while simultaneously blowing a ton of debris off of it. It was this debris that eventually coalesced into the moon we have today. I'm assuming that Giant Impact was at least portrayed in a scientifically accurate way up to this point.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Aug 05, 2012 6:14 am Bagheera wrote: it was an impact large enough to separate at least 20% of Earth's mass from the planet.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Aug 05, 2012 6:32 am Mr. Tines wrote: The Moon has approximately 1/4 Earth's diameter, 1/50 Earth's volume, but 1/80 Earth's mass (being mostly basalt, without a large iron core, so lower overall density).

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Sun Aug 05, 2012 6:48 am Bagheera wrote: Or, maybe Lilith was in a capsule inside of a larger moon, and the capsule, the "egg of Lilith" as it were, remained behind with her and is also referred to as the Black Moon. Dunno.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Sun Aug 05, 2012 7:19 am I always thought that it was Adam's arrival that created the moon, and that the Black Moon that we see in EoE was Lilith's entire vessel. So then this raises the question: What happened to Adam's supposedly Moon-sized vessel?

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Aug 05, 2012 9:49 am riffraff11235 wrote: I always thought that it was Adam's arrival that created the moon, and that the Black Moon that we see in EoE was Lilith's entire vessel. So then this raises the question: What happened to Adam's supposedly Moon-sized vessel?

[]:             Quote Post by BiQ » Mon Aug 06, 2012 12:53 am A stupid and uneducated thought: since the other Lance was actually part of 3I and fused with unit-01 to create the Tree thing, could the first one have been somehow part of 1I in somehow similar fashion? Edit: sorry, meant 1I.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Aug 06, 2012 5:30 am BiQ wrote: A stupid and uneducated thought: since the other Lance was actually part of 3I and fused with unit-01 to create the Tree thing, could the first one have been somehow part of 1I in somehow similar fashion?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Aug 07, 2012 11:55 am I'm going to point up at my previous post and ask if anyone has any ideas. riffraff11235 wrote: What happened to Adam's supposedly Moon-sized vessel?

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Tue Aug 07, 2012 2:30 pm Reichu wrote: The CI says that Lilith's Spear 'may have escaped from [her] and dispersed during First Impact', then goes on to say that it was probably destroyed because it hasn't been found. Well, that's kind of vague. How did it 'escape, disperse', and somehow get itself destroyed, exactly...?
